extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo attacked solo before the final Cat 3 climb at 5.2km to go, holding 30 seconds to win alone from a 33-rider break that gained 14+ minutes.
tactical_patterns: TRENTIN launched decisive attack just before Cat 3 climb began, not on steepest section, isolating chasers psychologically.
33-rider break gained 14 minutes with zero peloton interest, indicating GC teams deliberately conserving energy pre-Alps.
SAGAN abandoned race entirely 18 minutes back after learning heat-based time cut exemptions applied to peloton finishers.
VAN AVERMAET leading chase group suggests Quick-Step lacked commitment, allowing TRENTIN's European champion jersey psychological advantage.

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: Timed attack perfectly before climb start rather than on gradient, showing superior tactical positioning instinct.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Deliberately abandoned stage effort after confirming time cut exemption, prioritizing green jersey survival over stage hunting.
rider_signals.KING Ben: negative: Burned matches with two failed attacks then got dropped when TRENTIN countered, revealing poor energy management.
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: Led chase but failed to close 30-second gap, suggesting Quick-Step lacked motivation without sprint finish.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Led peloton across line ceremonially, confirming Deceuninck strategy of complete rest day before mountain stages.
